result:
1. trusts client header x-user-id for identity — violates Sec 2 rule 1 and Sec 5. Attacker sends any X-User-Id and moves another user's funds. Fix: remove header read; use req.user.sub set by auth_jwt middleware after JWT verification; also read org from req.user.org and authorize against :org_id.
2. no authentication enforced on route — handler bypasses the pipeline's auth_jwt step (Sec 3 step 5, Sec 5). Any unauthenticated caller can invoke transfer. Fix: register under the authenticated router so header_sanitize + rate_limit + body_size_guard + auth_jwt run; do not mount app.post outside the pipeline.
3. no org authorization — :org_id from URL not checked against the user's validated org/roles. Cross-org fund movement. Fix: assert req.user.org === req.params.org_id (or explicit membership+role check) before calling txSvc.
4. no input validation on from/to/amount — type, sign, precision, same-org invariant unchecked. Negative/NaN/huge amount, non-owned account IDs, SQLi-ish values reach tx-svc. Fix: schema-validate (zod/ajv); require amount integer >0, currency bounds, from≠to, both accounts belong to org.
5. missing idempotency key on money-moving POST — retries double-spend. Fix: require Idempotency-Key header, dedupe in tx-svc or at edge before dispatch.
6. route-specific rate limit missing — default 30/min/IP on POST is too loose for funds transfer and keyed by IP not user/org. Fix: add entry in config/rate_limits.json with tighter per-user+per-org limit; verify key includes region prefix per 2025-12 SEV2.
7. no error handling / never calls next(err) — Sec 11 forbids swallowing; a txSvc throw yields unhandled rejection and a raw 500 leaking internals (Sec 2 rule 6). Fix: try/catch → next(err); let src/edge/errorHandler.ts emit RFC 7807 problem+json with request_id.
8. response not RFC 7807 shape and no request_id — Sec 2 rule 6. `{ok:true}` on success is fine but error branch will violate. Fix: ensure errors go through central handler; success body should still be explicit (e.g., transfer_id) for audit.
9. body size not guarded at route — relies on pipeline body_size_guard; if route bypasses pipeline (see #2) a >2MB body DoSes the handler. Fix: mount under pipeline.
10. PII/financial fields likely logged via default access log — from/to/amount land in audit without redaction (Sec 2 rule 5, Sec 9). Fix: route audit_log through pii_redact; never log full req.body; log transfer_id + hashed account refs only.
11. no CODEOWNERS-gated review treatment — though route file may not be in the Sec 6 list, money-movement endpoint deserves equivalent human-approval gate. Fix: extend CODEOWNERS to cover src/edge/routes/transfer.ts (or wherever), require platform+payments sign-off.
12. missing tests — Sec 11 requires integration tests hitting real redis+pg; no happy/edge/error coverage. Fix: add integration tests for auth required, cross-org denied, negative amount rejected, idempotency replay, rate-limit trip, 429 shape.
13. trace/observability not wired — no explicit metric/label for transfer outcomes; Sec 7 expects per-route metrics and 100% trace on 5xx. Fix: add to always_trace_routes.json; emit counter with {result=ok|denied|invalid|error}.
14. uses app.post directly instead of versioned router module — Sec 3 pipeline is registered in src/edge/pipeline.ts; ad-hoc app.post risks ordering bugs (auth after dispatch). Fix: add handler to the router wired after header_sanitize and auth_jwt.
15. no audit-log entry for money movement — Sec 9 keeps 365-day audit; transfers must be in that stream with tamper-evident chain. Fix: emit structured audit event {actor, org, from_hash, to_hash, amount, transfer_id, request_id}.
